 {"id":"asupersync-16eos","title":"[AUDIT] Deep correctness audit: runtime/blocking_pool.rs + runtime/deadline_monitor.rs","notes":"VERDICT: SOUND — 0 bugs found

## blocking_pool.rs (1,329 lines)

### Concurrency primitives: 7 patterns
- AtomicUsize × 3: active_threads, busy_threads, pending_count
- AtomicU64 × 1: next_task_id
- AtomicBool × 3 patterns: shutdown (pool-level), cancelled (per-task), done (per-completion)
- Mutex<()> + Condvar × 2: pool parking (BlockingPoolInner), completion waiting (BlockingTaskCompletion)
- Mutex<Vec<ThreadJoinHandle>>: thread_handles
- crossbeam_queue::SegQueue: lock-free MPMC work queue

### CAS-guarded thread spawning (line 596-610)
spawn_thread_on_inner uses CAS loop on active_threads to atomically claim a slot before spawning. Prevents overshoot. Rollback on spawn failure (line 652-656).

### Worker loop lost-wakeup protection (line 698-772)
Queue checked under lock before condvar wait (lines 738, 762). Spawner holds lock for notify (lines 472-474). Serialization prevents: check empty → push → park → missed notification.

### try_claim_idle_retirement (line 678-694)
CAS loop to atomically decrement active_threads only if above min_threads. Prevents thundering herd.

### Panic handling (line 714-721)
catch_unwind(AssertUnwindSafe(task.work)) wraps execution. busy_threads decremented and signal_done called unconditionally. Prevents hanging waiters.

### retired_with_claim (line 639, 644-647)
blocking_worker_loop returns true if thread retired via CAS. Post-loop skips active_threads decrement (already done in CAS). Prevents double-decrement.

### BlockingTaskCompletion (line 157-206)
Mutex+Condvar with while-loop recheck. signal_done: store(true, Release) + lock + notify_all. wait: fast-path load(Acquire) + while(!done.load(Acquire)) condvar.wait. No lost wakeup.

### Ordering
- active_threads CAS: Relaxed in spawn (counter consistency only), AcqRel in retirement (synchronization)
- shutdown/cancelled/done: Acquire/Release (correct)
- busy_threads/pending_count/next_task_id: Relaxed (metrics/heuristics, correct)

## deadline_monitor.rs (1,154 lines)

### Concurrency primitives: ZERO
Pure &mut self data structure. Only external interaction: RwLock::read() on task.cx_inner (owned by task record). Guard dropped at line 344 before any self methods.

### check() dual interval gating (line 303-316)
Returns early only if BOTH logical time AND wall-clock time are below interval. Either advancing logical time (deterministic tests) or wall-clock time (real runtime) triggers scan. Correct.

### Warn-once semantics (line 401-425)
entry.warned = true before emission. Prevents duplicate warnings per task.

### Task cleanup (line 441)
retain() removes tasks no longer in scan. Prevents stale entry accumulation.

### DurationHistory::percentile_nanos (line 127-139)
Fixed-point rank (scaled × 1_000_000) avoids float imprecision for percentile calculation. Clamp 0.0-1.0, saturating_sub for idx.

### No bugs found in either module.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SapphireHill","created_at":"2026-02-15T06:01:29.525534871Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T06:53:43.332357690Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T06:53:43.332333104Z","close_reason":"done","external_ref":"bd-2kspi","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"asupersync-16euv","title":"[audit][codec] Fix LengthDelimitedCodec framing when num_skip retains header bytes","description":"src/codec/length_delimited.rs currently waits for and returns only frame_len bytes after parsing the length field, even when num_skip < header_len. In those configurations the decoder is supposed to retain some or all header bytes in the yielded frame, but the current state machine truncates the frame and misaligns the remaining buffer. Fix the state tracking so retained header bytes are counted in the pending frame length, add focused regressions for num_skip=0 and other retain-header configurations, and validate with rch-backed targeted cargo commands.","notes":"Implemented in src/codec/length_delimited.rs: decode now tracks retained bytes after num_skip instead of payload-only length, with checked overflow/underflow handling. Added regressions for num_skip=0 full-header retention and partial-header retention. Validation: `rch exec -- cargo check -p asupersync --lib` passed. `ubs --only=rust src/codec/length_delimited.rs` reported only generic test-file unwrap/assert warnings. `rch exec -- cargo test -p asupersync length_delimited -- --nocapture` is blocked by unrelated lib-test compile errors in src/time/wheel.rs (missing `next_occupied_before_wrap`). Repo-wide `cargo fmt --check` also currently fails from unrelated formatting drift in other files. Remote clippy timed out and tried to fail open locally; local fallback was cancelled to honor offload-only validation requirements.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"JadeTower","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:23:03.674532306Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T05:33:58.976394566Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","codec"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-16euv","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:23:19.863499169Z","created_by":"JadeTower"}]}
+{"id":"asupersync-16euv","title":"[audit][codec] Fix LengthDelimitedCodec framing when num_skip retains header bytes","description":"src/codec/length_delimited.rs currently waits for and returns only frame_len bytes after parsing the length field, even when num_skip < header_len. In those configurations the decoder is supposed to retain some or all header bytes in the yielded frame, but the current state machine truncates the frame and misaligns the remaining buffer. Fix the state tracking so retained header bytes are counted in the pending frame length, add focused regressions for num_skip=0 and other retain-header configurations, and validate with rch-backed targeted cargo commands.","notes":"Implemented in src/codec/length_delimited.rs: decode now tracks retained bytes after num_skip instead of payload-only length, with checked overflow/underflow handling. Remote clippy timed out and tried to fail open locally; local fallback was cancelled to honor offload-only validation requirements.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"MaroonBridge","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:23:03.674532306Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:41:23.768712215Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","codec"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-16euv","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:23:19.863499169Z","created_by":"JadeTower"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-16ij8","title":"[EPIC-PHASE] Phase 5 - DPOR and TLA+ Tooling","description":"## Overview
Phase 5 adds advanced testing and verification tools: Dynamic Partial Order Reduction (DPOR) for systematic schedule exploration and TLA+ model checking integration for formal verification.

## Goals
1. Systematic exploration of concurrent schedules
2. Optimal DPOR using Mazurkiewicz trace equivalence
3. TLA+ model extraction from runtime traces
4. Property-based testing integration

## Key Components

### 1. Schedule Exploration
Lab runtime already captures execution traces. Phase 5 adds systematic exploration:

```rust
pub struct ScheduleExplorer {
    known_traces: HashSet<TraceFingerprint>,
    pending_schedules: Vec<Schedule>,
    coverage: CoverageMetrics,
}

impl ScheduleExplorer {
    /// Run test under all "interesting" schedules
    pub fn explore<F: Fn(&mut LabRuntime)>(&mut self, test: F) -> ExplorationResult;
}
```

### 2. Dynamic Partial Order Reduction (DPOR)
DPOR avoids redundant schedule exploration:

**Key insight**: Two schedules are equivalent if they differ only in the order of *independent* operations. Independent operations commute; exploring both orderings is redundant.

**Mazurkiewicz traces**: Equivalence classes of schedules under commutativity. DPOR explores one representative per class.

**Algorithm**:
1. Run initial schedule, record trace
2. For each "race" (dependent operations), create alternative schedule
3. Prune schedules equivalent to already-explored traces
4. Repeat until no new schedules

### 3. Independence Relation
Define which operations commute:
| Op A | Op B | Independent? |
|------|------|--------------|
| Read x | Read y | Yes (any x, y) |
| Read x | Write y | Yes if x ≠ y |
| Write x | Write y | No if x = y |
| Send ch1 | Send ch2 | Yes if ch1 ≠ ch2 |
| Send ch | Recv ch | No (same channel) |

### 4. TLA+ Integration
Extract TLA+ models from runtime:

```rust
pub struct TlaExporter {
    // ...
}

impl TlaExporter {
    /// Export trace as TLA+ behavior
    pub fn export_trace(&self, trace: &Trace) -> TlaModule;
    
    /// Export runtime model (state machine)
    pub fn export_model(&self, runtime: &LabRuntime) -> TlaModule;
}
```

TLA+ enables:
- Temporal logic property checking (□ safety, ◇ liveness)
- Model checking with TLC
- Proof integration with TLAPS

### 5. Property-Based Testing
Integration with proptest/quickcheck:

```rust
#[proptest]
fn test_concurrent_counter(
    schedule: Schedule,
    operations: Vec<Op>,
) {
    LabRuntime::new(schedule).run(|| {
        // Execute operations
    });
    // Verify invariants
}
```

## Mathematical Foundation
From the spec:
- **Mazurkiewicz traces**: Partial orders over events, `≡` equivalence
- **DPOR correctness**: Explores all equivalence classes
- **Optimal DPOR**: Explores exactly one per class (no redundancy)

## Coverage Metrics
Track exploration completeness:
- Trace coverage: % of equivalence classes explored
- State coverage: % of reachable states visited
- Edge coverage: % of transitions exercised

## Dependencies
- Requires complete Phase 0-4 (all runtime features)
- Requires lab runtime trace capture
- Requires deterministic execution

## Testing the Testing Tools
Meta-testing:
- Known-buggy programs should have bugs found
- Known-correct programs should pass all schedules
- Coverage metrics should converge

## References
- asupersync_plan_v4.md: §7 Phase 5 (Tooling)
- DPOR papers (Flanagan, Godefroid)
- TLA+ (Lamport)
- PCT (probabilistic concurrency testing)
- Event structures and true concurrency

## Success Criteria
- Defines an independence relation over trace labels and a canonical trace normalization procedure.
- Schedule exploration explores one representative per equivalence class (optimal DPOR-class behavior).
- TLA+ export tooling can emit bounded models/behaviors from traces/state machine snapshots.
- Coverage metrics and reporting make exploration progress measurable and actionable.

Validation: rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-turquoise-session-lib cargo check -p asupersync --lib passed. Full --all-targets / lib-test runs are currently blocked by unrelated workspace-frontier failures (e.g. src/transport/aggregator.rs E0308, existing warnings in src/lab/runtime.rs under -D warnings).","created_at":"2026-03-07T23:52:16Z"},{"id":2450,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SageHill sub-slice complete: transport waiter fairness in channel sink/stream. Updated pop_next_queued_waiter in src/transport/sink.rs and src/transport/stream.rs to prune stale entries first and then wake FIFO (remove(0)) instead of LIFO pop, preventing starvation of older waiters under contention. Added deterministic regressions test_channel_sink_wakes_oldest_recv_waiter_first and test_channel_stream_wakes_oldest_send_waiter_first; updated stale-waiter test comments to reflect prune-on-pop behavior. Validation via rch: (1) timeout 420 rch exec -- cargo fmt --check => pass, (2) timeout 420 rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings => remote command exit=0 (rch then hung during artifact retrieval), (3) rch exec -- cargo test -p asupersync skips_stale_ -- --nocapture exposed unrelated pre-existing failures in src/transport/aggregator.rs E0308 at lines 1684 and 1706 and unrelated warning in src/lab/runtime.rs:1653.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:16:12Z"},{"id":2466,"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","author":"VioletLotus","text":"Working sub-slice in src/net/happy_eyeballs.rs: thread an explicit time getter through stagger timing, per-connection timeout, and overall timeout so resolver/lab paths can use deterministic time. Reserved src/net/happy_eyeballs.rs via Agent Mail; Agent Mail send_message is currently failing server-side with a transient DB error, so I’m recording status here. Validation is running via rch now.","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:01:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.1","title":"[audit][transport] Prevent PathSet ID reuse when mixing register() and create_path()","description":"src/transport/aggregator.rs currently leaves PathSet::next_id unchanged when callers manually register() a TransportPath. A subsequent create_path() can therefore reuse an existing PathId (for example after registering PathId(0) into a fresh PathSet) and silently overwrite the earlier entry in the path map. Fix by advancing the monotonic ID cursor on manual registration and add a regression test covering mixed register/create usage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"SwiftDune","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:54:20.802661784Z","created_by":"SwiftDune","updated_at":"2026-03-08T19:33:25.096978850Z","closed_at":"2026-03-08T19:33:25.096944707Z","close_reason":"Already implemented in-tree: src/transport/aggregator.rs::PathSet::register() now advances next_id to at least registered_id+1 via compare_exchange loop, and src/transport/aggregator.rs includes regression test test_path_set_register_advances_next_id covering mixed register/create usage. Closing as stale/currently-fixed after source audit; targeted rch validation is being refreshed separately.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","transport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:54:20.802661784Z","created_by":"SwiftDune","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Validate that this already-present fix clears the intended lib-level compile/lint surface via rch-backed targeted tests plus lib check/clippy, and only edit further if validation exposes a real remaining defect.","notes":"Validated the already-present local mutability fix for the try_join frontier without adding further code edits. Validation: rch exec -- cargo test -p asupersync --lib try_join_ -- --nocapture => PASS (6 tests passed: runtime::task_handle try_join_* plus related remote_handle_try_join_* coverage); rch exec -- cargo test -p asupersync --lib dynamic_lab_join_collects_all_leaves -- --nocapture => PASS; earlier current-tree lib gates also passed via rch: cargo check -p asupersync --lib -q => PASS, cargo clippy -p asupersync --lib --message-format short -- -D warnings => PASS. No additional code change was required from this bead beyond validating the already-present fix.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SageRaven","created_at":"2026-03-08T04:32:15.438236704Z","created_by":"SageRaven","updated_at":"2026-03-08T04:38:14.430133153Z","closed_at":"2026-03-08T04:38:14.430028759Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","runtime","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.10","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T04:32:15.438236704Z","created_by":"SageRaven","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.100","title":"[audit][signal] Preserve one recv() per recorded signal delivery","description":"src/signal/signal.rs tracks monotone delivery counts, but Signal::recv() currently sets seen_deliveries = current and returns a single Some(()) when current > seen_deliveries. If multiple deliveries are recorded before the next recv(), intermediate notifications are collapsed and lost despite the module docs saying no delivered signal notification is lost. Make recv consume exactly one pending delivery per await while preserving cancel safety, add focused regression coverage for multiple injected deliveries, and validate with rch-backed targeted cargo commands.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CrimsonCompass","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-03-10T18:23:17.501266861Z","created_by":"CrimsonCompass","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:23:17.501266861Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","signal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.100","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-10T18:23:17.501266861Z","created_by":"CrimsonCompass"}]}

Make recv consume exactly one pending delivery per await while preserving cancel safety, add focused regression coverage for multiple injected deliveries, and validate with rch-backed targeted cargo commands.","notes":"MaroonBridge taking ownership for implementation/validation now; coordinating via Agent Mail.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"MaroonBridge","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-03-10T18:23:17.501266861Z","created_by":"CrimsonCompass","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:36:25.082415362Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","signal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.100","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-10T18:23:17.501266861Z","created_by":"CrimsonCompass"}]}
+{"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.101","title":"[audit][runtime] Give BlockingPool timeout and idle bookkeeping a deterministic time getter","description":"src/runtime/blocking_pool.rs still uses ambient Instant::now()/thread::sleep() for BlockingTaskCompletion::wait_timeout, shutdown_and_wait timeout accounting, and worker idle-retirement timing. That makes timeout/retirement behavior hard to drive deterministically in focused tests and inconsistent with the repo's time-getter pattern already used in other timeout-sensitive modules. Thread explicit time-getter plumbing through the blocking-pool internals so timeout bookkeeping and idle-age calculations can be exercised without wall-clock sleeps, add targeted regressions for wait_timeout/shutdown timeout/idle retirement using synthetic clocks where practical, and validate with rch-backed targeted cargo commands.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"BrightHill","created_at":"2026-03-10T18:35:08.530013177Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:35:08.530013177Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","blocking-pool","determinism","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.101","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-10T18:35:08.530013177Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.102","title":"[audit][transport] Make collect_to_set yield cooperatively on always-ready symbol streams","description":"src/transport/stream.rs still implements CollectToSetFuture::poll as an unbounded loop over poll_next(). On an always-ready SymbolStream, one poll can drain the entire stream and monopolize the executor until EOF or error. Add a bounded per-poll drain budget with self-wake handoff so collect_to_set remains cooperative while preserving dedup/count semantics and early error propagation. Add focused regression coverage for an always-ready symbol stream and validate with rch-backed targeted cargo commands.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"MagentaBear","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-03-10T18:35:31.645901491Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-10T18:35:31.645901491Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","fairness","transport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.102","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-10T18:35:31.645901491Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

+{"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.104","title":"[audit][service] Make RetryFuture yield cooperatively on immediate retry loops","description":"src/service/retry.rs can burn through an arbitrary number of retries inside one poll when the cloned service is always-ready, the call future resolves synchronously, and the retry policy future is immediately ready (for example LimitedRetry over a fast-failing service).  {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.90","title":"[audit][stream] Make forward yield cooperatively on always-ready streams","description":"src/stream/forward.rs currently loops over stream.next().await and sender.try_send(...) until the input ends or the channel fills.  {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.92","title":"[audit][messaging] Make NatsClient::request timeouts deterministic and enforce them during reads","description":"src/messaging/nats.rs currently tracks request/reply deadlines with ambient Instant::now()/elapsed(), and the timeout check only runs before read_more().await.  {"id":"asupersync-1qfd0.93","title":"[audit][stream] Make Buffered/BufferUnordered yield cooperatively with large always-ready buffers","description":"src/stream/buffered.rs currently fills in-flight slots and polls queued futures in a single poll without any cooperative work budget. Add bounded per-poll admission and future-poll budgets with self-wake handoff so Buffered and BufferUnordered remain cooperative while preserving ordering/unordered semantics, completion behavior, and in-flight limits. Add focused regression coverage for large-limit always-ready cases and validate with rch-backed targeted cargo commands.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CopperMeadow","estimated_minutes":45,"created_at":"2026-03-10T17:12:39.159094560Z","created_by":"CopperMeadow","updated_at":"2026-03-10T17:26:41.391625723Z","closed_at":"2026-03-10T17:26:41.391601177Z","close_reason":"Implemented cooperative admission/poll budgets in src/stream/buffered.rs with regression coverage; rch buffered tests passed, lib check passed, file formatting clean; repo-wide fmt/clippy/check remain blocked by unrelated cx/listener/nats/collect frontier.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","fairness","stream"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1qfd0.93","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1qfd0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-10T17:12:39.159094560Z","created_by":"CopperMeadow"}]}
